<timestamp>Tuesday, Jun 2, 2026, 10:24 PM (UTC+10)</timestamp>
<user_query>
It is a nightmare making this team of agents the concierge has work. The routing is unreliable, keeping the state in order is just too hard. We did this to get more predictability, but that is not happening. I was concerned that 1 agent know all the different workflows would be too much to expect it to get right. But this is no better. The routing is probably our biggest problem, and even when

What do you recommend? I'm open to changing the plan ?

Can you tell me what happened here?
DEBUG ---------- OpenAI Responses Async Response Start -----------              
DEBUG ---------------------- Model: gpt-5.4 ----------------------              
DEBUG ========================== system ==========================              
DEBUG You coordinate a team of specialized AI agents to fulfill the user's      
      request. Delegate to members when their expertise or tools are needed. For
      straightforward requests you can handle directly ŌĆö including using your   
      own tools ŌĆö respond without delegating.                                   
                                                                                
      <team_members>                                                            
      <member id="design-steward" name="Design Steward">                        
        Role: Classify the claimed issue, run architecture design or revise an  
      existing design PR when required, and request human review. Does not      
      implement or call run_coder.                                              
      </member>                                                                 
      <member id="coder-steward" name="Coder Steward">                          
        Role: When the run requests implementation: create the implementation   
      branch if needed, assemble DoD (issue body + comment thread) and design   
      (committed arch-design file), then invoke run_coder and report the flow   
      summary.                                                                  
      </member>                                                                 
      <member id="discovery-steward" name="Discovery Steward">                  
        Role: Runs the discovery on a source repository, after checking that it 
      exsits and that a discovery hasn't already been run on itDelegate here    
      when the issue requires a discovery run.                                  
      </member>                                                                 
      <member id="validity-attester" name="Validity Attester">                  
        Role: Check whether a GitHub issue is still valid and relevant against  
      the current codebase on a given branch. Does not claim issues, run design,
      or implement.                                                             
      </member>                                                                 
      <member id="planner" name="Planner">                                      
        Role: Turn a caller-supplied product brief into an ordered list of      
      right-sized GitHub issues grounded in the repository. Does not file       
      issues, open PRs, or write detailed system architecture. Every delegation 
      must include brief (inline text), repo (owner/repo), discovery_ref (git   
      ref), and discovery_path (folder in ref); all four required ŌĆö forward     
      unchanged, no defaults.                                                   
        Description: Delegation input: brief = product direction as inline      
      markdown; repo = repository as owner/repo; discovery_ref = branch, tag, or
      commit SHA; discovery_path = path inside that ref to artifacts. Missing or
      malformed fields ŌåÆ halt with reason. Valid input ŌåÆ markdown plan with     
      milestones and flat ordered issues.                                       
      </member>                                                                 
      <member id="reimaginer" name="ReImaginer">                                
        Role: Read discovery artifacts at a caller-supplied location and produce
      three distinct AI-agent-first product reimaginings (Version A/B/C). Does  
      not file issues, produce roadmaps, or write architecture. Every delegation
      must include repo (owner/repo), discovery_ref (git ref), and              
      discovery_path (folder in ref); all three required ŌĆö forward unchanged, no
      defaults.                                                                 
        Description: Delegation input: repo = repository as owner/repo;         
      discovery_ref = branch, tag, or commit SHA where discovery artifacts live;
      discovery_path = path inside that ref to the artifacts folder. Missing or 
      malformed fields ŌåÆ halt with reason. Readable discovery ŌåÆ three versions  
      plus comparison and recommendation.                                       
      </member>                                                                 
      </team_members>                                                           
                                                                                
      <how_to_respond>                                                          
      You operate in coordinate mode. For requests that need member expertise,  
      select the best member(s), delegate with clear task descriptions, and     
      synthesize their outputs into a unified response. For requests you can    
      handle directly ŌĆö simple questions, using your own tools, or general      
      conversation ŌĆö respond without delegating.                                
                                                                                
      Delegation:                                                               
      - Match each sub-task to the member whose role and tools are the best fit.
      Delegate to multiple members when the request spans different areas of    
      expertise.                                                                
      - Write task descriptions that are self-contained: state the goal, provide
      relevant context from the conversation, and describe what a good result   
      looks like.                                                               
      - Use only the member's ID when delegating ŌĆö do not prefix it with the    
      team ID.                                                                  
                                                                                
      After receiving member responses:                                         
      - If a response is incomplete or off-target, re-delegate with clearer     
      instructions or try a different member.                                   
      - Synthesize all results into a single coherent response. Resolve         
      contradictions, fill gaps with your own reasoning, and add structure ŌĆö do 
      not simply concatenate member outputs.                                    
      </how_to_respond>                                                         
                                                                                
      <description>                                                             
      Two-phase backlog automation: pickup (claim issue, architecture-design    
      gate, human design review) and implement (run_coder on a coder branch).   
      Validity Attester checks whether a backlog issue is still relevant against
      the codebase before pickup proceeds. Discovery Steward runs the headless  
      discovery workflow and reports artifacts. Also includes Planner and       
      ReImaginer for product direction and delivery planning when explicitly    
      requested.                                                                
      </description>                                                            
                                                                                
      You are Project team leader responsible to build useful capabilities for a
      product and keep it working and maintainable. Your primary coordination   
      plane with the humans and other contributors is a Github Project board.   
      You have access to a team of architects, coders etc that whose            
      capabilities you should look up before you decide what to do.             
                                                                                
      # Rules you will not break:                                               
      1) **Everything that will change the code must have a Github issue for    
      it.** If a user asks you to build something without a GH issue reference  
      or for an issue that doesn't match the request, you must gather enough    
      information, then create the issue, ask the user to approve it and then   
      proceed.                                                                  
      2) **Break down and delegate to specialists on your team** Don't try to do
      something yourself if a specialist on your team is better suited to do it.
      3) **Don't assume, clarify and verify**                                   
      4) **Do not merge pull requests or close issues.** Supply links to the    
      user can easily do that themselves                                        
      5) **Do not act or advise outside your responsibility boundaries: - a     
      project board or repository**                                             
      6) **Mandatory validity check for issues older than 5 days**:- Not        
      recommended for new issues unless there is a good reason                  
                                                                                
      # Understanding the system you oversee                                    
      1) Discovery: The artifacts produced by discovery are stores in the repo  
      in a separate branch called `rebase-discovery` unless they get merged into
      main or deleted. They are under path `.rebaseai/discovery/artifacts`      
      2) Design creates a branch and PR and it is considered approved if a      
      comment indicating approval is found on the branch or PR. Merged PR is    
      also considered approval but often comments are used and these PRs are not
      merged but the design gets used by coder.                                 
      3) Coder creates a branch and PR.                                         
                                                                                
      ## Final output                                                           
      - A brief summary of actions you took.                                    
      - Any important surprises or unexpected issues along the way that the user
      should be aware of.                                                       
      - Any conclusions or outcomes that are important to the user.             
      - Any questions you have for the user to answer before you can proceed.   
                                                                                
      ## Deployment parameters                                                  
      - `architecture_design_branch_prefix`:                                    
      `architecture-design/issue-<issue_number>`                                
      - `coder_branch_prefix`: `coder/issue-<issue_number>` (default            
      implementation branch)                                                    
      - `arch_design_file_path`: `.rebaseai/arch-design/issue-<n>/design.md`    
      (committed design truth on arch branch; substitute issue number for <n>)  
      - `coding_artifacts_dir`: `.rebaseai/coding/<coder_branch>/` (coder       
      workflow artifacts)                                                       
      - `run_coder` optional `issue_number`: pass the backlog issue number if   
      available                                                                 
      - `human_review_marker`: `<!-- project-team:human-review -->`             
      - `implementation_marker`: `<!-- project-team:implementation -->`         
      - `start_branch` (architecture design + coder branch base): `main`        
      - `ready_statuses`: Todo, Ready                                           
      - `chore_labels`: chore, dependencies, ci, docs                           
      - `development_labels`: feature, enhancement, bug                         
      - `assignee_login`: unset (use the authenticated user for this session)   
      - `human_reviewers`: (none)                                               
                                                                                
      <additional_information>                                                  
      - Use markdown to format your answers.                                    
      </additional_information>                                                 
DEBUG =========================== user ===========================              
DEBUG # Project Board Dispatch Prompt                                           
                                                                                
      Pick **one** issue from the project board to process.                     
                                                                                
      ## Step 0 ŌĆö Fetch                                                         
                                                                                
      Query the board: use the project_list tool with query `status:"In         
      Progress",Ready`                                                          
                                                                                
      ## Step 1 ŌĆö Qualify                                                       
                                                                                
      An issue qualifies only if **all** are true:                              
                                                                                
      - Has no `rebaseai-working-*` label.                                      
      - Has no `rebaseai-failed-*` label.                                       
      - Has no `rebaseai-coded` label.                                          
                                                                                
      Then route:                                                               
                                                                                
      - **Coding** ŌĆö (has a label `rebaseai-designed`) AND ((a comment on the   
      issue or design PR indicating  design approval) OR (a merged design PR).  
      - **Design** ŌĆö if not already labelled `rebaseai-designed`.               
                                                                                
      ## Step 2 ŌĆö Global cap                                                    
                                                                                
      Count issues you have fetched that have any `rebaseai-working-*` label.   
      If the count is **Ōēź 3** (best effort calculation), stop. Do not call      
      `send_message` or `notifyError`.                                          
                                                                                
      ## Step 3 ŌĆö Pick one                                                      
                                                                                
      From qualified issues:                                                    
                                                                                
      1. Coding-routed before design-routed.                                    
      2. Within a route, oldest `created_at` first.                             
                                                                                
      Pick the top one. If none qualify, stop without notifying, bu tin your    
      output describe why each didn't qualify.                                  
                                                                                
      ## Step 4 ŌĆö Process                                                       
                                                                                
      1. Label the issue (using the issue_write tool):                          
         - Coding: add `rebaseai-working-coding`; remove `needs-arch-review` if 
      present.                                                                  
         - Design: add `rebaseai-working-design`.                               
      2. Delegate to the appropriate team member.                               
      3. Wait for completion.                                                   
      4. **Success:** remove the working label. add either rebaseai-designed or 
      rebaseai-coded label.                                                     
      5. **Failure:** remove the working label, add `rebaseai-failed-coding` or 
      `rebaseai-failed-design`, and post a comment stating processing failed.   
      Include only IDs the user can quote to RebaseAI (issue ID, session ID). No
      stack traces, tool output, or other internals.                            
                                                                                
      ## Step 5 ŌĆö Notify                                                        
                                                                                
      Call `send_message` on success or `notifyError` on failure, with a brief  
      one-line summary.                                                         
                                                                                
      ---                                                                       
                                                                                
      ### Notes                                                                 
                                                                                
      - The changed-issues list provided by the caller is **not** used. Drive   
      selection from the Step 0 fetch.                                          
                                                                                
                                                                                
      Project board URL: https://github.com/orgs/trilogy-group/projects/687     
                                                                                
      Changed issues (use these URLs for GitHub tool calls; do not guess the    
      repository):                                                              
      - https://github.com/trilogy-group/ab-agno/issues/181 ŌĆö project_manager:  
      input and output (labels updated)

## What happened here

YouŌĆÖre not imagining it ŌĆö this flow is behaving exactly like a brittle router, not a reliable workflow engine.

From the logs:

- The **project-board poll** did run, but either:
  - detected no meaningful change and skipped dispatch, or
  - fetched items and found **no qualifying issue** (everything had `rebaseai-working-*` or `rebaseai-coded`), so it intentionally did nothing.
- In the **concierge session**, there was **no active workflow state**, so the router kept delegating to `product-guide`.
- The user message ŌĆ£IŌĆÖm sold, I want to rebaseŌĆØ did **not trigger discovery**, because the product guide required repo details first.
- ŌĆ£How is it going?ŌĆØ got routed as generic chat, so it repeated intake instructions.
- Then ŌĆ£you said discovery had startedŌĆØ triggered a correction: **discovery had not started**.

So the system didnŌĆÖt crash. It followed its own rules, but those rules produce dead-end loops under normal user behavior.

## My recommendation (opinionated)

You should change the plan.  
**Current multi-agent conversational routing is the wrong control plane for this stage.**

Best approach for reliability:

- Replace free-form routing with a **deterministic state machine** for concierge:
  - `INTAKE -> DISCOVERY -> REIMAGINE -> PLAN -> PROVISION`
  - one active state, explicit transition guards, explicit resume behavior.
- Keep specialists, but only as **workers invoked by state**, not as peers deciding flow by prompt interpretation.
- For short/ambiguous user messages (ŌĆ£how is it going?ŌĆØ, ŌĆ£continueŌĆØ), always route to **current state owner**, never re-classify from scratch.
- Add a required structured intake form (repo URL, product, pain points, goals) and block transitions until complete.
- Treat labels/project-board polling as **execution status**, not core orchestration logic.

## Why this is better

- You remove the biggest failure mode: LLM intent/routing drift.
- You get predictable transitions and recoverable sessions.
- You can test transitions with fixtures (no model variance needed).
- Users stop getting ŌĆ£weŌĆÖre waiting for inputŌĆØ loops when they expect progress.

If you want, I can sketch the exact state model and transition table next (events, guards, and failure handling) so you can implement this incrementally without rewriting everything.

## My opinion: stop using a silent router. Make concierge the responder.

The logs show exactly why the "silent router ŌåÆ product-guide" pattern is failing:

- Every user turn requires **two LLM hops** (router classifies ŌåÆ product-guide answers), each with full system prompts. That's where the latency, cost, and drift compound.
- The router has **no real decision to make** in 80% of turns ŌĆö there's no active workflow, so it always picks `product-guide`. It's a coin flip dressed up as orchestration.
- The product-guide ends up doing the actual conversational work anyway, but **without authority** to start workflows. So it keeps saying "send me your repo" instead of just starting discovery.
- When the user said "you said discovery had started", neither layer had ground truth ŌĆö the router has no workflow state, and product-guide can only describe stages, not move between them.

You picked routing because you feared one agent knowing all workflows would be too much. **The evidence in this session says the opposite is true**: the routing layer is the unreliable part, not the domain knowledge.

## What concierge should be

A **single coordinator agent** that:

1. **Owns the conversation directly.** It responds to the user. No silent delegation for chitchat, intake, status, or clarifications.
2. **Holds explicit workflow state** as a typed object it reads and writes deterministically (not via prompt interpretation). `current_stage`, `repo`, `goals`, `last_event`, `pending_input`.
3. **Invokes specialists as tools, not as peers.** `run_discovery(repo)`, `run_reimaginer(artifacts_ref)`, `run_planner(proposal_id)`, `run_provisioner(target_repo)`. Specialists return structured results; concierge decides what to say and what's next.
4. **Uses a deterministic transition guard** in code (not the prompt) for `INTAKE ŌåÆ DISCOVERY ŌåÆ REIMAGINE ŌåÆ PLAN ŌåÆ PROVISION`. The LLM can't skip or invent a stage.
5. **Treats short/ambiguous user input** ("yes", "continue", "how's it going?") as a stage-scoped event ŌĆö concierge looks at `current_stage` and answers from that context. No re-classification.

## Why one agent is fine here

Your original fear was scope. But the **workflows are sequential and mutually exclusive** ŌĆö a user is in exactly one stage at a time. That's not "one agent knowing everything" territory; that's a small state machine with five tool calls. The cognitive load on the model is tiny once stage is explicit in the prompt.

The specialists still exist ŌĆö they do the heavy lifting (scanning, generating artifacts, etc.). But they're **workers behind tools**, not chat participants competing for routing decisions.

## What to keep from current design

- The specialist agents themselves (discovery, reimaginer, planner, provisioner) ŌĆö their internal prompts and workflows are reusable.
- Project-board polling ŌĆö orthogonal to concierge, keep it.
- Memory manager ŌĆö useful, keep it.

## What to drop

- The router/coordinate split for concierge.
- `product-guide` as a separate agent. Its content becomes part of concierge's own system prompt.
- The "team_history_context" injection pattern ŌĆö concierge has direct history because it's the one talking.

Want me to sketch the concrete concierge agent shape (system prompt skeleton, state schema, tool signatures) so you can compare it to what's in `agents/concierge/` today?

Also, each workflow has many steps, lots of UI cards to pick from. I am skeptical that concierge will make all the right selections, especially iff we give it one giant prompt all the time. Maybe we can selectively inject relevant workflow details based on the stage of the workflow, and that will reduce its context and improve accuracy.

Second: How about we make the other members have query type functions where the concierge can ask them clarifying questions about mechanics if it doesn't knwo enough to answer the user. Eg: how exactly does discovery work, will it create any files in my repo? 

Another option is to make the team transparent to the user and hand off. SO the user is aware that they are now talking to the Rethinker, and the rethinker can hand off.

I read the design doc. It's actually a solid document ŌĆö the failures in the log aren't from the design being wrong in spirit, they're from a specific weak spot: **the idle / pre-workflow state**, where Product Guide and the router are both guessing without authority. Now to your three options:

## Option 1: Stage-conditional prompt injection

**Yes, do this.** This is the right instinct and it's compatible with everything in the design doc.

Today every router turn carries the full member roster, full product framing, all routing rules. That's wasted tokens *and* the model has to re-decide context every turn. Better:

- Router prompt = thin, stable: routing rules + current workflow state. Nothing about Reimaginer's card flow.
- **Member prompts get composed from blocks based on stage.** Reimaginer's prompt only loads "iterate over proposals" instructions when `active_workflow.stage == proposing`. When it shifts to "awaiting approval", swap in the approval-card guidance.
- Langfuse already supports prompt composition via the `@@@langfusePrompt@@@` references. Use stage as a label or compose at build time per stage.

This isn't an alternative to route mode ŌĆö it's an upgrade to the members.

## Option 2: Query functions between members

**I'd push back on this.** It looks helpful but it adds three problems:

1. **It re-creates the routing problem inside concierge.** To ask Reimaginer "do you write files to the repo?", concierge already has to know it's a Reimaginer question. If it knows that much, it should just route the turn.
2. **Two LLM hops per answer**, with the second one (concierge interpreting specialist's structured answer) being the new drift surface.
3. **Where do the answers live?** They're stable product facts ("does discovery write to my repo?"). Those belong in **Product Info** (a Langfuse prompt block) that the responding member loads when needed ŌĆö not in a query API.

If you want specialists to be sources of truth about themselves, do it as **shared prompt fragments** (one per specialist: `rebaseai/discovery-mechanics`, `rebaseai/reimaginer-mechanics`), and inject the relevant one into whoever answers. No extra agent call.

## Option 3: Transparent handoff with named specialists

**This is the one I'd actually adopt.** Three reasons grounded in the log:

1. The user said *"you said discovery had started"* ŌĆö that's a **state-tracking failure on the user's side**, not the system's. Because the team is opaque, the user has no anchor for who said what or where they are. A named handoff ("I'm Discovery ŌĆö I'll walk through scanning your repo now") gives the user a mental model that matches the underlying state machine.
2. It collapses the routing problem. Once the user is "talking to Reimaginer", short replies, "yes", "continue" obviously go to Reimaginer. No silent re-classification.
3. It legitimizes the off-owner turn. "One sec, let me hand you to Product Guide for that ŌĆö Reimaginer will pick back up after." Users tolerate explicit context switches; they hate invisible ones.

The design doc's ADR-2 (Product Guide as a member, opaque to user) was the wrong call given the complexity you're now describing. With named handoffs you can also **let the leader answer simple meta-turns directly** ("which step are we on?") because it's no longer pretending to be invisible.

## My recommended synthesis

Keep route mode + WorkflowStateStore. Change four things:

1. **Make handoffs visible** in UI: small "Talking to: Reimaginer" header, with a "back to overview" affordance. Drop the "silent router" fiction.
2. **Stage-conditional member prompts**, composed from Langfuse fragments by stage. Skinny router prompt stays.
3. **Drop Product Guide as a separate member**; let the named concierge own greetings, intake, status, and meta-questions when no workflow is active. This is the catch-all the doc tried to avoid ŌĆö but with explicit stages, "no active workflow" is itself a stage, with bounded scope (greet, collect repo, hand off to Discovery). Not a catch-all anymore.
4. **No member-to-member query API.** Stable mechanics live as shared prompt fragments, loaded by whoever owns the turn.

This keeps the parts of the design that are working (one owner per turn, typed workflow state, verbatim input) and fixes the parts the log proved are not (idle-state routing roulette, opaque handoffs, monolithic member prompts).

Want me to draft the revised ADRs (specifically: ADR-2 superseded, plus new ADRs for transparent handoff and stage-conditional prompts) so you have a concrete change to review against the existing design doc?
