"""Composition root for the Concierge route-mode team."""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Any

from agno.agent import Agent
from agno.db.base import ComponentType
from agno.db.postgres import PostgresDb
from agno.team import Team
from agno.team.mode import TeamMode

from agents.concierge.constants import (
    CONCIERGE_TEAM_ID,
    DISCOVERY_MEMBER_ROLE,
    PLANNER_MEMBER_ROLE,
    PROVISIONER_MEMBER_ROLE,
    REIMAGINER_MEMBER_ROLE,
)
from agents.concierge.context import concierge_team_context_kwargs
from agents.concierge.langfuse_prompts import fetch_router_prompt_for_settings
from agents.concierge.member_wiring import (
    attach_concierge_discovery_member,
    attach_concierge_member,
    attach_concierge_planner_member,
    attach_concierge_provisioner_member,
    attach_concierge_reimaginer_member,
)
from agents.concierge.memory import build_concierge_memory_manager
from agents.concierge.session_tools import build_prior_session_toolkit
from agents.concierge.settings import ConciergeSettings, load_concierge_settings
from agents.concierge.streaming import wrap_concierge_team_arun
from agents.concierge.workflow_state import WorkflowStateStore
from agents.langfuse_prompts import SessionInstructionsCache, session_langfuse_instructions
from agno_platform.models.presets import MODEL_MEDIUM


def _router_instructions(
    *,
    cache: SessionInstructionsCache,
    settings: ConciergeSettings,
) -> Any:
    base_fetch = session_langfuse_instructions(
        cache=cache,
        cache_key="concierge-router",
        fetch_text=lambda: fetch_router_prompt_for_settings(settings),
    )

    def instructions(
        agent: Any = None,
        team: Any = None,
        session_state: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
        run_context: Any = None,
    ) -> str:
        del agent, run_context
        body = base_fetch(team=team, session_state=session_state)
        workflow_block = WorkflowStateStore.format_for_instructions(
            WorkflowStateStore.read(session_state)
        )
        return f"{body}\n\n# Workflow State\n{workflow_block}"

    return instructions


def build_concierge_team(
    db: PostgresDb,
    *,
    concierge_member_agent: Agent,
    provisioner_agent: Agent,
    discovery_agent: Agent,
    reimaginer_agent: Agent,
    planner_agent: Agent,
    session_instructions_cache: SessionInstructionsCache,
    settings: ConciergeSettings | None = None,
) -> Team:
    """Build the Concierge team (route mode): router + Concierge + specialists."""
    resolved = settings or load_concierge_settings()

    attach_concierge_member(concierge_member_agent)

    attach_concierge_provisioner_member(provisioner_agent)
    if provisioner_agent.role is None:
        provisioner_agent.role = PROVISIONER_MEMBER_ROLE

    attach_concierge_discovery_member(discovery_agent)
    if discovery_agent.role is None:
        discovery_agent.role = DISCOVERY_MEMBER_ROLE

    attach_concierge_reimaginer_member(reimaginer_agent)
    if reimaginer_agent.role is None:
        reimaginer_agent.role = REIMAGINER_MEMBER_ROLE

    attach_concierge_planner_member(planner_agent)
    if planner_agent.role is None:
        planner_agent.role = PLANNER_MEMBER_ROLE

    team = Team(
        id=CONCIERGE_TEAM_ID,
        name="RebaseAI Concierge",
        description=(
            "Single conversational entry point for RebaseAI. Routes each turn to "
            "Concierge or a specialist with shared session history, workflow state, "
            "and visible handoffs."
        ),
        mode=TeamMode.route,
        determine_input_for_members=False,
        model=MODEL_MEDIUM,
        db=db,
        members=[
            concierge_member_agent,
            provisioner_agent,
            discovery_agent,
            reimaginer_agent,
            planner_agent,
        ],
        tools=[build_prior_session_toolkit(db)],
        memory_manager=build_concierge_memory_manager(db),
        instructions=_router_instructions(
            cache=session_instructions_cache,
            settings=resolved,
        ),
        markdown=True,
        **concierge_team_context_kwargs(),
    )
    wrap_concierge_team_arun(team, settings=resolved)
    return team


def ensure_concierge_team_persisted(db: Any, team: Team) -> None:
    """Persist the Concierge team for Studio ``/components`` listing."""
    if db.get_component(CONCIERGE_TEAM_ID, component_type=ComponentType.TEAM) is not None:
        return
    team.save()

"""Unit tests for Concierge route team build."""

from __future__ import annotations

import time
from typing import cast
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

import pytest
from agno.db.postgres import PostgresDb
from agno.team.mode import TeamMode

from agents.concierge.build import build_concierge_team
from agents.concierge.constants import (
    CONCIERGE_MEMBER_AGENT_ID,
    CONCIERGE_TEAM_ID,
    DISCOVERY_WORKFLOW_OWNER_ID,
    PLANNER_WORKFLOW_OWNER_ID,
    REIMAGINER_WORKFLOW_OWNER_ID,
)
from agents.concierge.context import CONCIERGE_NUM_HISTORY_RUNS
from agents.concierge.concierge_member import build_concierge_member_agent
from agents.concierge.discovery_agent import build_discovery_agent
from agents.concierge.planner_agent import build_planner_agent
from agents.concierge.reimaginer_agent import build_reimaginer_agent
from agents.langfuse_prompts import SessionInstructionsCache
from agents.provisioner.constants import PROVISIONER_AGENT_ID
from agents.provisioner_agent import build_provisioner_agent


def _fake_fetch(_settings, name: str, **kwargs) -> str:
    return f"prompt:{name}"


@pytest.fixture
def concierge_team(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch):
    monkeypatch.setenv("LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY", "pk-test")
    monkeypatch.setenv("LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY", "sk-test")
    monkeypatch.setenv("LANGFUSE_BASE_URL", "https://langfuse.example.com")
    cache = SessionInstructionsCache(max_entries=10)
    now = time.monotonic()
    cache.set("concierge-router", now, "Router instructions.")
    db = cast(PostgresDb, MagicMock())
    with patch("agents.concierge.prompt_composer._fetch_prompt_text", side_effect=_fake_fetch):
        concierge_member = build_concierge_member_agent(db, session_instructions_cache=cache)
        provisioner = build_provisioner_agent(
            db,
            session_instructions_cache=cache,
            instructions="Provisioner test instructions.",
        )
        discovery = build_discovery_agent(db, session_instructions_cache=cache)
        reimaginer = build_reimaginer_agent(db, session_instructions_cache=cache)
        planner = build_planner_agent(db, session_instructions_cache=cache)
        return build_concierge_team(
            db,
            concierge_member_agent=concierge_member,
            provisioner_agent=provisioner,
            discovery_agent=discovery,
            reimaginer_agent=reimaginer,
            planner_agent=planner,
            session_instructions_cache=cache,
        )


def test_team_structure(concierge_team) -> None:
    assert concierge_team.id == CONCIERGE_TEAM_ID
    assert concierge_team.mode == TeamMode.route
    member_ids = {m.id for m in concierge_team.members or []}
    assert member_ids == {
        CONCIERGE_MEMBER_AGENT_ID,
        PROVISIONER_AGENT_ID,
        DISCOVERY_WORKFLOW_OWNER_ID,
        REIMAGINER_WORKFLOW_OWNER_ID,
        PLANNER_WORKFLOW_OWNER_ID,
    }


def test_router_has_prior_session_tool(concierge_team) -> None:
    tool_names: set[str] = set()
    for toolkit_or_fn in concierge_team.tools or []:
        for t in getattr(toolkit_or_fn, "tools", []):
            if name := getattr(t, "name", None):
                tool_names.add(name)
    assert "get_prior_workflow_state" in tool_names


def _member_tool_names(member) -> set[str]:
    names: set[str] = set()
    for toolkit_or_fn in member.tools or []:
        tools = getattr(toolkit_or_fn, "tools", None)
        if tools is not None:
            for t in tools:
                if name := getattr(t, "name", None):
                    names.add(name)
    return names


def test_concierge_member_has_workflow_tool_after_team_build(concierge_team) -> None:
    concierge = next(m for m in concierge_team.members or [] if m.id == CONCIERGE_MEMBER_AGENT_ID)
    assert "update_workflow_state" in _member_tool_names(concierge)


def test_provisioner_has_workflow_tool_after_team_build(concierge_team) -> None:
    provisioner = next(m for m in concierge_team.members or [] if m.id == PROVISIONER_AGENT_ID)
    names = _member_tool_names(provisioner)
    assert "update_workflow_state" in names
    assert "render_provision_pipeline_card" not in names


def test_discovery_agent_has_workflow_and_run_tools_after_team_build(concierge_team) -> None:
    discovery = next(m for m in concierge_team.members or [] if m.id == DISCOVERY_WORKFLOW_OWNER_ID)
    names = _member_tool_names(discovery)
    assert "update_workflow_state" in names
    assert "run_discovery" in names


def test_reimaginer_has_workflow_and_render_tools_after_team_build(concierge_team) -> None:
    reimaginer = next(
        m for m in concierge_team.members or [] if m.id == REIMAGINER_WORKFLOW_OWNER_ID
    )
    names = _member_tool_names(reimaginer)
    assert "update_workflow_state" in names
    assert "render_reimagine_proposals_card" in names


def test_planner_has_workflow_and_render_tools_after_team_build(concierge_team) -> None:
    planner = next(m for m in concierge_team.members or [] if m.id == PLANNER_WORKFLOW_OWNER_ID)
    names = _member_tool_names(planner)
    assert "update_workflow_state" in names
    assert "render_plan_card" in names


def test_team_and_members_have_user_memory_and_history(concierge_team) -> None:
    assert concierge_team.update_memory_on_run is True
    assert concierge_team.add_memories_to_context is True
    assert concierge_team.add_history_to_context is True
    assert concierge_team.add_team_history_to_members is True
    assert concierge_team.num_history_runs == CONCIERGE_NUM_HISTORY_RUNS
    assert concierge_team.num_team_history_runs == CONCIERGE_NUM_HISTORY_RUNS
    for member in concierge_team.members or []:
        assert member.update_memory_on_run is True
        assert member.add_memories_to_context is True
        assert member.add_history_to_context is True
        assert member.num_history_runs == CONCIERGE_NUM_HISTORY_RUNS


def test_router_instructions_include_workflow_state(concierge_team) -> None:
    assert callable(concierge_team.instructions)
    body = concierge_team.instructions(
        session_state={
            "concierge_workflow": {
                "active_workflow": {
                    "owner": "provisioner-agent",
                    "status": "active",
                    "goal": "Provision",
                    "stage": "intake",
                    "resume_hint": "",
                    "last_user_visible_event": "",
                    "updated_at": "2026-06-01T00:00:00Z",
                }
            }
        }
    )
    assert "Router instructions." in body
    assert "provisioner-agent" in body
    assert "intake" in body

"""Unit tests for active_member_changed streaming events."""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import cast
from unittest.mock import MagicMock

import pytest
from agno.models.response import ToolExecution

from agents.concierge.constants import (

def _settings() -> ConciergeSettings:

def test_maybe_emit_writes_session_and_returns_event() -> None:
    session: dict = {}
    event = maybe_emit_active_member_changed(
        session,
        member_id=DISCOVERY_WORKFLOW_OWNER_ID,
        workflow_state=WorkflowStateStore.read(session),
        team_id="concierge-team",
        team_name="RebaseAI Concierge",
        run_id="run-1",
        session_id="sess-1",
    )
    assert event is not None
    assert event.name == ACTIVE_MEMBER_CHANGED_EVENT_NAME
    assert getattr(event, "member_id") == DISCOVERY_WORKFLOW_OWNER_ID
    assert session[ACTIVE_MEMBER_SESSION_KEY] == DISCOVERY_WORKFLOW_OWNER_ID


def test_maybe_emit_skips_when_unchanged() -> None:
    session = {ACTIVE_MEMBER_SESSION_KEY: CONCIERGE_MEMBER_AGENT_ID}
    event = maybe_emit_active_member_changed(
        session,
        member_id=CONCIERGE_MEMBER_AGENT_ID,
        workflow_state=WorkflowStateStore.read(session),
        team_id="t",
        team_name="n",
        run_id=None,
        session_id=None,
    )
    assert event is None


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_stream_emits_handoff_on_delegate() -> None:
    team = MagicMock()
    team.id = "concierge-team"
    team.name = "RebaseAI Concierge"
    session: dict = {}

    delegate_tool = ToolExecution(
        tool_call_id="tc-1",
        tool_name="delegate_task_to_member",
        tool_args={"member_id": DISCOVERY_WORKFLOW_OWNER_ID},
    )

    async def fake_stream():
        yield ToolCallStartedEvent(
            tool=delegate_tool,
            team_id="concierge-team",
            team_name="RebaseAI Concierge",
            run_id="run-1",
            session_id="sess-1",
        )
        yield RunCompletedEvent(
            team_id="concierge-team",
            team_name="RebaseAI Concierge",
            content="done",
            session_state=session,
        )

    events = [
        e
        async for e in concierge_team_stream(
            team,
            fake_stream(),
            user_message="go",
            settings=_settings(),
            session_state=session,
            workflow_state_before=WorkflowStateStore.read(session),
        )
    ]

    handoffs = [e for e in events if isinstance(e, CustomEvent)]
    assert len(handoffs) == 1
    assert handoffs[0].name == ACTIVE_MEMBER_CHANGED_EVENT_NAME
    assert session[ACTIVE_MEMBER_SESSION_KEY] == DISCOVERY_WORKFLOW_OWNER_ID
    assert events[-1].event == TeamRunEvent.run_completed.value

"""Unit tests for Concierge team streaming helpers."""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import cast
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch

import pytest
from agno.models.response import ToolExecution
from agno.run.agent import RunOutput
from agno.run.team import RunCompletedEvent, TeamRunEvent

from agents.concierge.render_tools import RENDER_SUGGESTED_PROMPTS_TOOL
from agents.concierge.settings import ConciergeSettings
from agents.concierge.streaming import (
    build_suggested_prompt_tool_events,
    concierge_team_stream,
    should_inject_product_completion_suggestions,
    workflow_completed_this_turn,
)
from agents.concierge.workflow_state import (
    LastCompletedWorkflow,
    WorkflowState,
    WorkflowStateStore,
)


def _settings() -> ConciergeSettings:
    return ConciergeSettings(
        langfuse_public_key="pk",
        langfuse_secret_key="sk",
        langfuse_base_url="https://langfuse.example.com",
        router_langfuse_prompt_name="ux/concierge-router",
        concierge_member_langfuse_prompt_name="ux/concierge",
        discovery_langfuse_prompt_name="ux/discovery",
        reimaginer_langfuse_prompt_name="ux/reimaginer",
        planner_langfuse_prompt_name="ux/planner",
        langfuse_prompt_name="ux/concierge",
        default_terminal_url="http://localhost:7681/",
        demo_terminal_base_url="https://demo.rebase.ai/terminal",
    )


def test_workflow_completed_detection() -> None:
    before = WorkflowState()
    after = WorkflowState(
        last_completed_workflow=LastCompletedWorkflow(
            owner="provisioner-agent",
            goal="Provision",
            outcome="done",
        )
    )
    assert workflow_completed_this_turn(state_before=before, state_after=after)


def test_product_completion_injection_gate() -> None:
    session = {
        WorkflowStateStore.session_key: {
            "last_completed_workflow": {
                "owner": "provisioner-agent",
                "goal": "Provision",
                "outcome": "Terminal ready",
                "recommended_next_steps": [],
            }
        }
    }
    completion = RunCompletedEvent(
        team_id="concierge-team",
        content="All set.",
        session_state=session,
    )
    assert should_inject_product_completion_suggestions(completion, state_before=WorkflowState())


def test_build_suggested_prompt_tool_events_team_fields() -> None:
    completion = RunCompletedEvent(team_id="concierge-team", team_name="RebaseAI Concierge")
    events = build_suggested_prompt_tool_events(completion, ["A", "B", "C"])
    started = events[0]
    assert started.team_id == "concierge-team"
    assert started.tool is not None
    assert started.tool.tool_name == RENDER_SUGGESTED_PROMPTS_TOOL


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_stream_skips_suggestions_without_workflow_completion() -> None:
    team = MagicMock()
    team.id = "concierge-team"
    team.name = "RebaseAI Concierge"
    team.model = None
    session: dict = {}

    async def fake_stream():
        yield RunCompletedEvent(
            team_id="concierge-team",
            team_name="RebaseAI Concierge",
            content="Hello.",
            session_state=session,
        )

    with patch(
        "agents.concierge.streaming.generate_suggested_prompts",
        new_callable=AsyncMock,
    ) as generate:
        events = [
            event
            async for event in concierge_team_stream(
                team,
                fake_stream(),
                user_message="hi",
                settings=_settings(),
                session_state=session,
                workflow_state_before=WorkflowState(),
            )
        ]

    generate.assert_not_called()
    assert events[-1].event == TeamRunEvent.run_completed.value
    assert not any(e.event == TeamRunEvent.tool_call_started.value for e in events)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_stream_yields_completion_suggestions_on_workflow_complete() -> None:
    team = MagicMock()
    team.id = "concierge-team"
    team.name = "RebaseAI Concierge"
    team.model = None
    session = {
        WorkflowStateStore.session_key: {
            "last_completed_workflow": {
                "owner": "provisioner-agent",
                "goal": "Provision",
                "outcome": "Terminal ready",
                "recommended_next_steps": [],
            }
        }
    }

    async def fake_stream():
        yield RunCompletedEvent(
            team_id="concierge-team",
            team_name="RebaseAI Concierge",
            content="All set.",
            session_state=session,
        )

    with patch(
        "agents.concierge.streaming.generate_suggested_prompts",
        new_callable=AsyncMock,
        return_value=["One", "Two", "Three"],
    ):
        events = [
            event
            async for event in concierge_team_stream(
                team,
                fake_stream(),
                user_message="done",
                settings=_settings(),
                session_state=session,
                workflow_state_before=WorkflowState(),
            )
        ]

    assert events[-1].event == TeamRunEvent.run_completed.value
    assert any(e.event == TeamRunEvent.tool_call_started.value for e in events)

"""Unit tests for Concierge Langfuse prompt resolution."""

from __future__ import annotations

from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

from agents.concierge.langfuse_prompts import (
    fetch_langfuse_prompt,
    fetch_router_prompt_for_settings,
    resolve_langfuse_prompt_references,
)
from agents.concierge.settings import ConciergeSettings


def test_resolve_langfuse_prompt_references() -> None:
    client = MagicMock()

    def get_prompt(name: str, *, label: str, cache_ttl_seconds: int | None = None) -> str:
        del label, cache_ttl_seconds
        return f"BODY:{name}"

    client.get_prompt.side_effect = get_prompt
    body = resolve_langfuse_prompt_references(
        "Intro\n@@@langfusePrompt:name=rebaseai/product-info|label=production@@@\nEnd",
        client,
    )
    assert "BODY:rebaseai/product-info" in body


def _make_settings() -> ConciergeSettings:
    return ConciergeSettings(
        langfuse_public_key="pk",
        langfuse_secret_key="sk",
        langfuse_base_url="https://langfuse.example.com",
        router_langfuse_prompt_name="ux/concierge-router",
        concierge_member_langfuse_prompt_name="ux/concierge",
        discovery_langfuse_prompt_name="ux/discovery",
        reimaginer_langfuse_prompt_name="ux/reimaginer",
        planner_langfuse_prompt_name="ux/planner",
        langfuse_prompt_name="ux/concierge",
        default_terminal_url="http://localhost:7681/",
        demo_terminal_base_url="https://demo.rebase.ai/terminal",
    )


def test_fetch_router_prompt_for_settings_delegates() -> None:
    fake_client = MagicMock()
    fake_client.get_prompt.return_value = MagicMock(compile=lambda **_: "router-body")
    settings = _make_settings()

    with patch(
        "agents.concierge.langfuse_prompts.get_langfuse_client",
        return_value=fake_client,
    ) as mock_get_client:
        body = fetch_router_prompt_for_settings(settings)

    mock_get_client.assert_called_once_with(
        public_key=settings.langfuse_public_key,
        secret_key=settings.langfuse_secret_key,
        base_url=settings.langfuse_base_url,
    )
    fake_client.get_prompt.assert_called_once()
    call_args = fake_client.get_prompt.call_args
    assert call_args.args[0] == settings.router_langfuse_prompt_name
    assert body == "router-body"


def test_fetch_langfuse_prompt_passes_variables() -> None:
    class _PromptWithCompile:
        def compile(self, **kwargs: str) -> str:
            return f"terminal={kwargs['default_terminal_url']}"

    client = MagicMock()
    client.get_prompt.return_value = _PromptWithCompile()
    settings = ConciergeSettings(
        langfuse_public_key="pk",
        langfuse_secret_key="sk",
        langfuse_base_url="https://langfuse.example.com",
        router_langfuse_prompt_name="ux/concierge-router",
        concierge_member_langfuse_prompt_name="ux/concierge",
        discovery_langfuse_prompt_name="ux/discovery",
        reimaginer_langfuse_prompt_name="ux/reimaginer",
        planner_langfuse_prompt_name="ux/planner",
        langfuse_prompt_name="ux/concierge",
        default_terminal_url="http://localhost:7681/",
        demo_terminal_base_url="https://demo.rebase.ai/terminal",
    )
    body = fetch_langfuse_prompt(client, "ux/concierge-router", settings=settings)
    assert body == "terminal=http://localhost:7681/"

content.text:
Implemented the full **Concierge Transparent Handoff** plan. Summary:

## Architecture changes

- **Kept `TeamMode.route`** with `determine_input_for_members=False`.
- **Replaced Product Guide** with front-of-house member `concierge-agent` ([`agents/concierge/concierge_member.py`](agents/concierge/concierge_member.py)).
- **Added `stage`** to workflow state; specialists set it via `update_workflow_state`.
- **Stage-conditional prompts** via [`agents/concierge/prompt_composer.py`](agents/concierge/prompt_composer.py): `spine` + `stage/<stage>` + workflow block.
- **Transparent handoff**: [`agents/concierge/streaming.py`](agents/concierge/streaming.py) emits `CustomEvent(name="active_member_changed", ...)` and writes `session_state["active_member_id"]` when the delegated member changes.
- **Dropped per-turn fallback suggestion chips**; only **workflow-completion** chips remain.
- **UI ownership unchanged**: specialists still call their own `render_*` tools; Concierge only has `render_suggested_prompts`.

## Langfuse

Published 38 prompts to `production` via [`scripts/publish_concierge_prompts.py`](scripts/publish_concierge_prompts.py) (router, concierge, all spine/mechanics/stage fragments).

## Key files touched

| Area | Files |
|------|--------|
| State | `workflow_state.py`, `workflow_tools.py` |
| Constants/settings | `constants.py`, `settings.py`, `.env`, `example.env` |
| Team build | `build.py`, `member_wiring.py`, `app/main.py` |
| Specialists | `discovery_agent.py`, `reimaginer_agent.py`, `planner_agent.py` + provisioner instructions in `main.py` |
| Streaming | `streaming.py` |
| Tests | New: `test_workflow_state_stage`, `test_prompt_composer`, `test_streaming_handoff`, `test_concierge_member`; updated build/streaming/agent tests |

## AGUI contract for frontend

Listen for team `CustomEvent` with `name == "active_member_changed"`. Payload includes:

- `member_id`, `member_name`, `previous_member_id`, `handoff_reason`

(AGUI wraps it as `CustomEvent(name="CustomEvent", value={...full event dict...})` ŌĆö read `value.name` or the top-level fields from `to_dict()`.)

## Validation

- `ruff check` / `ruff format --check`: pass  
- `pytest tests/unit`: **500 passed**

**Manual AGUI smoke** (handoff pill + stage-scoped prompts in DEBUG logs) is still on you when the app is running locally.

input.contents:
#!/usr/bin/env sh
# Spot-check AgentOS API from inside the agentos-api container (or with AGENTOS_URL).
set -eu

BASE_URL="${AGENTOS_URL:-http://127.0.0.1:9900}"
TOKEN="${AGENTOS_INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN:-}"
if [ -z "$TOKEN" ]; then
  echo "AGENTOS_INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN is not set" >&2
  exit 1
fi

AUTH="Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN"
THREAD="spot-$(date +%s)"
RUN="run-$THREAD"

echo "=== GET $BASE_URL/status ==="
curl -s -w "\nHTTP:%{http_code}\n" -H "$AUTH" "$BASE_URL/status"

echo "=== POST $BASE_URL/agui (concierge smoke) ==="
BODY=$(cat <<EOF
{
  "threadId": "$THREAD",
  "runId": "$RUN",
  "messages": [
    {
      "id": "msg-1",
      "role": "user",
      "content": "Say hello in five words or fewer."
    }
  ],
  "tools": [],
  "context": [],
  "state": {},
  "forwardedProps": {
    "user_id": "b866c4e3-5972-477d-b6c3-510a58b84d41"
  }
}
EOF
)

curl -s -N --max-time 120 -w "\n\nHTTP:%{http_code}\n" \
  -H "$AUTH" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "$BODY" \
  "$BASE_URL/agui" | head -c 6000

echo ""
echo "=== POST $BASE_URL/teams (list requires teams:read) ŌĆö skip if 403 ==="

content.text:
## API container status

**`agentos-api` is healthy.** It has been up, finished startup (scheduler poller, tool registration), and serves **`GET /docs` ŌåÆ 200** and **`GET /status` ŌåÆ 200** from inside the container.

**Reachability note:** From this devcontainer, `curl http://127.0.0.1:9900` gets **connection refused** (port publish is on the Docker host, not always visible here). Spot checks were run with `docker exec agentos-api curl http://127.0.0.1:9900/...`, which matches how the app is bound in `compose.dev.yaml` (`UVICORN_PORT=9900`).

---

## Auth: whatŌĆÖs in `.env` vs a UI bearer token

`.env` does **not** contain a ready-made `eyJŌĆ”` bearer for the Agno UI. It has:

| Variable | Role |
|----------|------|
| **`JWT_VERIFICATION_KEYS`** | RS256 **public** PEM(s) for **verifying** tokens from os.agno.com (UI) |
| **`SUPABASE_JWKS_FILE`** | Local Supabase **public** JWKS (ES256) |
| **`AGENTOS_INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN`** | Internal service auth (scheduler-style), not the UI JWT |

A default Supabase HS256 test token (`super-secret-jwt-token-with-at-least-32-characters-long`) returns **401** (`alg value is not allowed`) because the running config expects **ES256 + RS256**, not HS256.

To impersonate the UI you need a real UI-issued RS256 JWT, or an ES256 token signed with the **private** key that matches `keys/local-supabase-jwks.json` (only the public JWK is in the repo). For smoke tests, **`AGENTOS_INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN`** is the practical choice.

---

## POST spot checks (authenticated)

### 1. `GET /status` ŌĆö OK

```json
{"status":"available"}
```

HTTP **200**

### 2. `POST /agui` (Concierge AGUI path) ŌĆö OK

Valid body shape (AG-UI / camelCase): `threadId`, `runId`, `messages[]` with `id` + `role` + `content`, `tools: []`, `context: []`, `state`, **`forwardedProps`** (required, can be `{}`).

Observed SSE (abbreviated):

- `RUN_STARTED`
- Router `delegate_task_to_member` ŌåÆ **`concierge-agent`**
- Assistant text: **`spot-check-ok`**
- `RUN_FINISHED`

HTTP **200**, stream ~1.6KB for a short prompt.

### 3. `POST /teams/concierge-team/runs` ŌĆö OK

Multipart form (not JSON):

```bash
-F 'message=Reply with exactly: spot-check-ok'
-F 'stream=false'
-F 'session_id=team-spot-2'
```

Response **~12KB JSON**, `"content":"spot-check-ok"`, `team_id":"concierge-team"`, member **`concierge-agent`** completed. HTTP **200**.

### 4. `GET /components?limit=2` ŌĆö expected 403

Internal token scopes include `teams:run` but not **`components:read`** ŌåÆ **403** (RBAC working as designed).

---

## Summary

| Check | Result |
|--------|--------|
| Container running | Yes |
| App startup | Complete |
| `GET /status` | 200 |
| `POST /agui` | 200, concierge team runs, expected reply |
| `POST /teams/concierge-team/runs` | 200, `spot-check-ok` |
| UI JWT from `.env` alone | Not available (verification keys only) |
| Host `:9900` from devcontainer | Refused (use `docker exec` or host network) |

No `RUN_ERROR` or auth failures on the successful runs above. This AGUI run routed to **`concierge-agent`** without a separate `active_member_changed` custom event in the captured stream (handoff events fire when the active member **changes** during streaming, not on every delegate).

If you want, I can add a tiny `scripts/spot_check_api.sh` that runs these checks inside the container and optionally documents how to paste a real UI `Bearer` token for RS256 testing.

[REDACTED]

</user_query>

content.text:
## AGUI contract changes (Concierge transparent handoff)

### Unchanged (no UI work required)

- **Route:** still `POST /agui` with standard `RunAgentInput` (`threadId`, `runId`, `messages`, `tools`, `context`, `state`, `forwardedProps`).
- **Core stream:** `RUN_STARTED` ŌåÆ `TEXT_MESSAGE_*` / `TOOL_CALL_*` ŌåÆ `RUN_FINISHED` (same AG-UI protocol).
- **What the user sees in chat:** still only the **selected memberŌĆÖs** text (`show_members_responses=false`); router delegation is visible as `delegate_task_to_member` tool calls, not as extra assistant bubbles.
- **UI cards:** still driven by specialist `render_*` tool results (`RepoIntakeForm`, `DiscoveryProgressCard`, etc.) ŌĆö no new card types from this slice.

---

### New stream event: `active_member_changed`

**Purpose:** Drive a ŌĆ£Talking to: ŌĆ”ŌĆØ pill and optional handoff banner **before** the specialistŌĆÖs first text delta.

**When it fires**

- At most **once per turn**, only when the routed member **changes** vs `session_state.active_member_id`.
- Emitted right after `delegate_task_to_member` starts (or on first member content if delegate was missed), **before** that memberŌĆÖs prose.
- **Not** emitted if the router delegates to the same member already active (e.g. another turn with Concierge still active).

**Wire format (SSE `data:` JSON)**

Agno maps team `CustomEvent` ŌåÆ AG-UI like this:

```json
{
  "type": "CUSTOM",
  "name": "CustomEvent",
  "value": {
    "name": "active_member_changed",
    "member_id": "discovery-agent",
    "member_name": "Discovery",
    "previous_member_id": "concierge-agent",
    "handoff_reason": "starting Discovery",
    "team_id": "concierge-team",
    "team_name": "RebaseAI Concierge",
    "run_id": "...",
    "session_id": "..."
  }
}
```

**UI handling**

1. Listen for `type === "CUSTOM"`.
2. Treat as handoff when `value?.name === "active_member_changed"` (donŌĆÖt rely on top-level `name === "active_member_changed"` ŌĆö that stays `"CustomEvent"`).
3. Use `value.member_name` for the pill; `value.handoff_reason` for a one-line banner (examples: `"starting Discovery"`, `"side question"`, `"continuing Reimaginer"`, `"returning to Concierge"`).

**Member IDs** (for labels / routing logic)

| `member_id` | Display name |
|-------------|----------------|
| `concierge-agent` | Concierge |
| `discovery-agent` | Discovery |
| `reimaginer-agent` | Reimaginer |
| `planner-agent` | Planner |
| `provisioner-agent` | Provisioner |

Legacy alias: `product-guide` ŌåÆ use **`concierge-agent`** (same agent).

---

### Session `state` additions (not new SSE types)

Persisted in AGUI `state` / team `session_state` (round-trip on each request):

| Key | Meaning |
|-----|--------|
| `active_member_id` | Who the UI should show as ŌĆ£activeŌĆØ after the last handoff |
| `concierge_workflow` | Existing workflow blob; **`active_workflow.stage`** is new (string, specialist sub-stage e.g. reimaginer `proposing`) |

Use these on reconnect if you donŌĆÖt want to wait for the next `active_member_changed`.

---

### Removed / narrowed behavior

- **Per-turn fallback suggestion chips** injected mid-stream are **gone**.
- **`render_suggested_prompts`** still appears **only after a workflow completes** (end-of-run tool events), same as before for completion chips.

---

### Optional: donŌĆÖt infer speaker from stream alone

You can still read `member_id` on some team chunks, but the supported contract is **`active_member_changed`** + `active_member_id` in session state, not parsing delegate tool args in the UI.

---

### Quick UI checklist

def active_member_changed_payload(
    *,
    member_id: str,
    previous_member_id: str | None,
    workflow_state: WorkflowState,
    team_id: str,
    team_name: str,
    run_id: str | None,
    session_id: str | None,
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Handoff dict for AGUI CUSTOM.value (see build_active_member_changed_event)."""
    return {
        "name": ACTIVE_MEMBER_CHANGED_EVENT_NAME,
        "member_id": member_id,
        "member_name": MEMBER_DISPLAY_NAMES.get(member_id, member_id),
        "previous_member_id": previous_member_id or "",
        "handoff_reason": _handoff_reason(
            previous_member_id=previous_member_id,
            member_id=member_id,
            workflow_state=workflow_state,
        ),
        "team_id": team_id,
        "team_name": team_name,
        "run_id": run_id,
        "session_id": session_id,
    }


def build_active_member_changed_event(
    *,
    member_id: str,
    previous_member_id: str | None,
    workflow_state: WorkflowState,
    team_id: str,
    team_name: str,
    run_id: str | None,
    session_id: str | None,
) -> CustomEvent:
    """Emit as Agno CustomEvent; AGUI maps it to type=CUSTOM with value=<payload>.

    Agno's CustomEvent uses a non-dataclass __init__, so to_dict() raises and the
    AGUI translator falls back to ``content``. Without ``content``, value is omitted
    from SSE (exclude_none) and the UI never sees active_member_changed.
    """
    payload = active_member_changed_payload(
        member_id=member_id,
        previous_member_id=previous_member_id,
        workflow_state=workflow_state,
        team_id=team_id,
        team_name=team_name,
        run_id=run_id,
        session_id=session_id,
    )
    return CustomEvent(content=payload, **payload)

def test_handoff_event_serializes_for_agui_custom_value() -> None:
    """AGUI must receive CUSTOM.value; empty value breaks the UI speaker pill."""
    from ag_ui.core import EventType
    from ag_ui.encoder import EventEncoder
    from agno.os.interfaces.agui.utils import EventBuffer, _create_events_from_chunk

    handoff = build_active_member_changed_event(
        member_id=DISCOVERY_WORKFLOW_OWNER_ID,
        previous_member_id=CONCIERGE_MEMBER_AGENT_ID,
        workflow_state=WorkflowStateStore.read({}),
        team_id="concierge-team",
        team_name="RebaseAI Concierge",
        run_id="run-1",
        session_id="sess-1",
    )
    agui_events, _, _ = _create_events_from_chunk(
        handoff,
        message_id="",
        message_started=False,
        event_buffer=EventBuffer(),
    )
    custom = [e for e in agui_events if e.type == EventType.CUSTOM]
    assert len(custom) == 1
    assert custom[0].value is not None
    assert custom[0].value["name"] == ACTIVE_MEMBER_CHANGED_EVENT_NAME
    assert custom[0].value["member_id"] == DISCOVERY_WORKFLOW_OWNER_ID
    assert custom[0].value["member_name"] == "Discovery"

    encoded = EventEncoder().encode(custom[0])
    assert '"value"' in encoded
    assert ACTIVE_MEMBER_CHANGED_EVENT_NAME in encoded
    assert DISCOVERY_WORKFLOW_OWNER_ID in encoded


def test_maybe_emit_writes_session_and_returns_event() -> None:

content.text:
## Root cause

Your RCA is right: delegation happened, but the UI contract event was **broken on the wire**.

AgnoŌĆÖs `CustomEvent` uses a hand-rolled `__init__` that only `setattr`s kwargs. It does **not** initialize dataclass fields like `created_at`, so `to_dict()` raises:

`AttributeError: 'CustomEvent' object has no attribute 'created_at'`

The AGUI translator (`agno/os/interfaces/agui/utils.py`) then does:

1. Try `chunk.to_dict()` ŌåÆ **fails**
2. Fall back to `chunk.content` ŌåÆ was **`None`**
3. Build `CustomEvent(name="CustomEvent", value=None)`
4. SSE encode with `exclude_none=True` ŌåÆ **`value` dropped**

So the client sees:

```json
{"type":"CUSTOM","name":"CustomEvent"}
```

with no `value`, and the UI correctly stays on Concierge.

The handoff **was** likely emitted in the team stream; it just wasnŌĆÖt serializable.

---

## Fix

In `build_active_member_changed_event` we now put the full handoff payload in **`content`** (the AGUI fallback path) and mirror the same fields on the event for tests:

```python
payload = { "name": "active_member_changed", "member_id": ..., ... }
return CustomEvent(content=payload, **payload)
```

After fix, SSE looks like:

```json
{
  "type": "CUSTOM",
  "name": "CustomEvent",
  "value": {
    "name": "active_member_changed",
    "member_id": "provisioner-agent",
    "member_name": "Provisioner",
    "previous_member_id": "concierge-agent",
    "handoff_reason": "starting Provisioner",
    ...
  }
}
```

---

## Tests

Added `test_handoff_event_serializes_for_agui_custom_value` ŌĆö runs `_create_events_from_chunk` + `EventEncoder` and asserts `value.name` / `member_id` survive. All 4 handoff tests pass.

---

## What you need to do

1. **Redeploy/restart** `agentos-api` with this change.
2. **No UI contract change** ŌĆö keep listening for `type === "CUSTOM"` and `value?.name === "active_member_changed"`.
3. Re-run the Provisioner flow; you should see `value.member_name: "Provisioner"` before provisioning copy.

If you still see an empty `CUSTOM` after deploy, that frame is probably a **different** Agno custom event (not our handoff); filter on `value?.name === "active_member_changed"`.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
hy did it fail siltently. I don't like silent failures. Are we not cathin erros or are we hiding them, or do we have stupid fallbacks?
</user_query>

Tracing the exact code paths where the failure was swallowed versus surfaced.

[REDACTED]

YouŌĆÖre right to be annoyed ŌĆö this wasnŌĆÖt ŌĆ£the UI missed something.ŌĆØ It was **three layers treating a broken payload as success**, with **no log line anywhere**.

## What actually failed

Our wrapper **did** emit a handoff `CustomEvent` on the team stream. The bug was **serialization to AGUI SSE**, not ŌĆ£handoff logic never ran.ŌĆØ

## Where the silence comes from (stacked)

### 1. Agno AGUI translator ŌĆö bare `except`, no log

In upstream `agno/os/interfaces/agui/utils.py`:

```434:449:/home/vscode/.cache/uv/archive-v0/vxfIgdb1MjQOMuEU/agno/os/interfaces/agui/utils.py
    elif chunk.event == RunEvent.custom_event:
        try:
            custom_event_name = chunk.__class__.__name__
        except Exception:
            custom_event_name = chunk.event

        try:
            custom_event_value = chunk.to_dict()
        except Exception:
            custom_event_value = chunk.content  # type: ignore

        custom_event = CustomEvent(name=custom_event_name, value=custom_event_value)
        events_to_emit.append(custom_event)
```

For our handoff event:

- `to_dict()` **raises** (`CustomEvent`ŌĆÖs `__init__` never sets `created_at`, etc.).
- The `except` **swallows** that with **no** `log_warning` / `log_error`.
- Fallback is `chunk.content`, which we had left **`None`**.
- It still **appends** a CUSTOM event ŌĆö so the run continues as if nothing went wrong.

ThatŌĆÖs a **stupid fallback** in the sense you mean: ŌĆ£if serialization fails, use `content`ŌĆØ with no check that `content` is non-empty, and no visibility when both paths are bad.

### 2. AG-UI encoder ŌĆö `exclude_none=True` drops `value`

```28:32:/home/vscode/.cache/uv/archive-v0/EpUA4sypcMVc6KjK/ag_ui/encoder/encoder.py
    def _encode_sse(self, event: BaseEvent) -> str:
        return f"data: {event.model_dump_json(by_alias=True, exclude_none=True)}\n\n"
```

`value=None` ŌåÆ field omitted ŌåÆ wire frame is only `type` + `name`. ThatŌĆÖs intentional for JSON hygiene, but combined with (1) it **hides** the failure from anyone reading SSE.

### 3. Our code ŌĆö no contract check before emit

We built `CustomEvent(name=..., member_id=..., ...)` and assumed that would become `CUSTOM.value`. We didnŌĆÖt:

- set `content` (the only reliable path when `to_dict()` fails),
- assert the payload after building,
- or log if the event wouldnŌĆÖt survive AGUI encoding.

So we werenŌĆÖt **hiding** errors in a `try/except`; we **never knew** Agno would fail `to_dict()` and fall back to empty `content`. Unit tests only checked Python attributes on the in-memory event, not the AGUI wire shape.

### 4. HTTP / run semantics ŌĆö still ŌĆ£successŌĆØ

The API returns **200** and valid SSE. An empty CUSTOM is syntactically fine. The team run completes. Only the **UI contract** breaks ŌĆö nothing in the server stack treats that as an error.

---

## Summary

| Layer | Behavior |
|--------|----------|
| **Our wrapper** | Emitted handoff; no wire validation |
| **Agno AGUI** | `except Exception` ŌåÆ `content`; no log |
| **AG-UI encoder** | Drops `null` fields silently |
| **HTTP** | 200, stream OK |

So: **mostly upstream fallback + encoder omission**, plus **our missing validation**, not a single ŌĆ£we catch and hideŌĆØ in concierge ŌĆö but the **effect** is the same as silent failure.

---

## What we fixed

`build_active_member_changed_event` now sets **`content=payload`** so AgnoŌĆÖs fallback path carries the real handoff dict, and we added a test that runs `_create_events_from_chunk` + `EventEncoder` and asserts `value.name` / `member_id` are on the wire.

---

## If you want fewer silent failures going forward

Reasonable hardening (we can add if you want):

1. **`build_active_member_changed_event`**: `if not payload.get("name"): raise RuntimeError(...)` ŌĆö fail fast at emit time in dev.
2. **Optional log** in our stream wrapper when yielding a custom event whose `content` is missing (belt-and-suspenders).
3. **Upstream Agno** (separate PR): log when `to_dict()` fails and `content` is empty instead of emitting a hollow CUSTOM.

We **should not** copy AgnoŌĆÖs bare `except Exception: pass` pattern in our code; the fix is **make the contract path reliable** + **test the wire format**, not another silent fallback.

I can add (1) + a debug log on (2) in a small follow-up if you want fail-loud in dev without waiting for an Agno release.

[REDACTED]

input.contents:
"""Typed AGUI handoff contract for active_member_changed (fail loud, no silent fallbacks)."""

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass
from typing import Any, Literal

from ag_ui.core import EventType
from ag_ui.encoder import EventEncoder
from agno.os.interfaces.agui.utils import EventBuffer, _create_events_from_chunk
from agno.run.team import CustomEvent

from agents.concierge.constants import ACTIVE_MEMBER_CHANGED_EVENT_NAME

_REQUIRED_WIRE_KEYS = frozenset(
    {
        "name",
        "member_id",
        "member_name",
        "previous_member_id",
        "handoff_reason",
        "team_id",
        "team_name",
        "run_id",
        "session_id",
    }
)


class HandoffWireError(RuntimeError):
    """active_member_changed cannot be encoded for AGUI; the run must fail."""


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class ActiveMemberChangedValue:
    """UI contract for CUSTOM.value when value.name is active_member_changed."""

    name: Literal["active_member_changed"]
    member_id: str
    member_name: str
    previous_member_id: str
    handoff_reason: str
    team_id: str
    team_name: str
    run_id: str | None
    session_id: str | None

    def to_wire_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return asdict(self)


def parse_active_member_changed_value(data: object) -> ActiveMemberChangedValue:
    """Validate an AGUI CUSTOM.value payload; raise HandoffWireError if invalid."""
    if not isinstance(data, dict):
        raise HandoffWireError(
            f"active_member_changed value must be a dict, got {type(data).__name__}"
        )
    missing = _REQUIRED_WIRE_KEYS - data.keys()
    if missing:
        raise HandoffWireError(
            f"active_member_changed value missing keys: {sorted(missing)}"
        )
    name = data["name"]
    if name != ACTIVE_MEMBER_CHANGED_EVENT_NAME:
        raise HandoffWireError(
            f"expected name={ACTIVE_MEMBER_CHANGED_EVENT_NAME!r}, got {name!r}"
        )
    member_id = data["member_id"]
    if not isinstance(member_id, str) or not member_id.strip():
        raise HandoffWireError("active_member_changed member_id must be a non-empty string")
    member_name = data["member_name"]
    if not isinstance(member_name, str) or not member_name.strip():
        raise HandoffWireError("active_member_changed member_name must be a non-empty string")
    return ActiveMemberChangedValue(
        name=ACTIVE_MEMBER_CHANGED_EVENT_NAME,
        member_id=member_id.strip(),
        member_name=member_name.strip(),
        previous_member_id=str(data["previous_member_id"]),
        handoff_reason=str(data["handoff_reason"]),
        team_id=str(data["team_id"]),
        team_name=str(data["team_name"]),
        run_id=data["run_id"] if isinstance(data["run_id"], str) else None,
        session_id=data["session_id"] if isinstance(data["session_id"], str) else None,
    )


def resolve_agui_custom_wire_value(event: CustomEvent) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Mirror Agno's AGUI custom-event translation (without swallowing errors)."""
    try:
        wire = event.to_dict()
    except Exception as exc:
        content = getattr(event, "content", None)
        if not isinstance(content, dict):
            raise HandoffWireError(
                "CustomEvent.to_dict() failed and content is not a dict; "
                "AGUI would emit CUSTOM without value"
            ) from exc
        return content
    return wire


def enforce_active_member_changed_agui_contract(event: CustomEvent) -> ActiveMemberChangedValue:
    """Ensure this CustomEvent survives Agno AGUI translation and SSE encoding.

    Raises HandoffWireError so the /agui run fails instead of emitting a hollow CUSTOM frame.
    """
    wire = resolve_agui_custom_wire_value(event)
  # If to_dict succeeded, handoff fields may live under content ŌĆö reject ambiguous shapes.
    if wire.get("name") != ACTIVE_MEMBER_CHANGED_EVENT_NAME:
        content = wire.get("content")
        if isinstance(content, dict) and content.get("name") == ACTIVE_MEMBER_CHANGED_EVENT_NAME:
            wire = content
        else:
            raise HandoffWireError(
                "AGUI wire value does not contain active_member_changed at the top level"
            )

    parsed = parse_active_member_changed_value(wire)

    agui_events, _, _ = _create_events_from_chunk(
        event,
        message_id="",
        message_started=False,
        event_buffer=EventBuffer(),
    )
    custom_events = [e for e in agui_events if e.type == EventType.CUSTOM]
    if len(custom_events) != 1:
        raise HandoffWireError(
            f"expected exactly one AGUI CUSTOM event, got {len(custom_events)}"
        )
    agui_custom = custom_events[0]
    if agui_custom.value is None:
        raise HandoffWireError("AGUI CUSTOM.value is None after translation")

    encoded = EventEncoder().encode(agui_custom)
    if '"value"' not in encoded:
        raise HandoffWireError("AGUI SSE frame omits value (would break UI speaker pill)")

    return parse_active_member_changed_value(agui_custom.value)


def build_handoff_custom_event(value: ActiveMemberChangedValue) -> CustomEvent:
    """Build a CustomEvent and verify it satisfies the AGUI contract before returning."""
    payload = value.to_wire_dict()
    event = CustomEvent(content=payload, **payload)
    enforce_active_member_changed_agui_contract(event)
    return event

from agents.concierge.constants import (
    ACTIVE_MEMBER_CHANGED_EVENT_NAME,
    ACTIVE_MEMBER_SESSION_KEY,
    CONCIERGE_MEMBER_AGENT_ID,
    MEMBER_DISPLAY_NAMES,
    PRODUCT_INFO_PROMPT_NAME,
)

def active_member_changed_payload(
    *,
    member_id: str,
    previous_member_id: str | None,
    workflow_state: WorkflowState,
    team_id: str,
    team_name: str,
    run_id: str | None,
    session_id: str | None,
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Handoff dict for AGUI CUSTOM.value (see build_active_member_changed_event)."""
    return {
        "name": ACTIVE_MEMBER_CHANGED_EVENT_NAME,
        "member_id": member_id,
        "member_name": MEMBER_DISPLAY_NAMES.get(member_id, member_id),
        "previous_member_id": previous_member_id or "",
        "handoff_reason": _handoff_reason(
            previous_member_id=previous_member_id,
            member_id=member_id,
            workflow_state=workflow_state,
        ),
        "team_id": team_id,
        "team_name": team_name,
        "run_id": run_id,
        "session_id": session_id,
    }


def build_active_member_changed_event(
    *,
    member_id: str,
    previous_member_id: str | None,
    workflow_state: WorkflowState,
    team_id: str,
    team_name: str,
    run_id: str | None,
    session_id: str | None,
) -> CustomEvent:
    """Emit as Agno CustomEvent; AGUI maps it to type=CUSTOM with value=<payload>.

    Agno's CustomEvent uses a non-dataclass __init__, so to_dict() raises and the
    AGUI translator falls back to ``content``. Without ``content``, value is omitted
    from SSE (exclude_none) and the UI never sees active_member_changed.
    """
    payload = active_member_changed_payload(
        member_id=member_id,
        previous_member_id=previous_member_id,
        workflow_state=workflow_state,
        team_id=team_id,
        team_name=team_name,
        run_id=run_id,
        session_id=session_id,
    )
    return CustomEvent(content=payload, **payload)

def build_active_member_changed_event(
    *,
    member_id: str,
    previous_member_id: str | None,
    workflow_state: WorkflowState,
    team_id: str,
    team_name: str,
    run_id: str | None,
    session_id: str | None,
) -> CustomEvent:
    """Build and verify an AGUI handoff event; raises HandoffWireError if encoding would fail."""
    value = ActiveMemberChangedValue(
        name=ACTIVE_MEMBER_CHANGED_EVENT_NAME,
        member_id=member_id,
        member_name=MEMBER_DISPLAY_NAMES.get(member_id, member_id),
        previous_member_id=previous_member_id or "",
        handoff_reason=_handoff_reason(
            previous_member_id=previous_member_id,
            member_id=member_id,

        ),
        team_id=team_id,
        team_name=team_name,
        run_id=run_id,
        session_id=session_id,
    )
    return build_handoff_custom_event(value)

content.text:
You're right to push on this. HereŌĆÖs the honest picture and what we changed.

## Is there type safety there?

**In AgnoŌĆÖs AGUI path: basically no.**  
`CustomEvent` is a bag of `setattr` kwargs. The translator does `try: to_dict() except: content` with **no typed contract** and no failure mode. ThatŌĆÖs untyped runtime soup, not something mypy can save you from at the framework boundary.

**In our handoff path: we added a real boundary** ŌĆö not logs, **hard failures**.

## What ŌĆ£silentŌĆØ meant (three different things)

| Layer | What happens |
|--------|----------------|
| **Agno** | `except Exception` ŌåÆ use `content`; never re-raise |
| **AG-UI encoder** | `exclude_none=True` drops `value` if itŌĆÖs `None` |
| **HTTP** | Still 200 + valid SSE ŌĆö ŌĆ£successŌĆØ with a broken contract |

None of that is type safety; itŌĆÖs **fallback + omission**, which is another way to hide bugs.

We werenŌĆÖt adding our own silent `except` in concierge; we **trusted** that `CustomEvent(**kwargs)` would survive AgnoŌĆÖs encoder. It didnŌĆÖt.

## What we did instead of logs

New module `agents/concierge/agui_handoff.py`:

1. **`ActiveMemberChangedValue`** ŌĆö frozen dataclass with `Literal["active_member_changed"]` and required fields. Wrong/missing fields ŌåÆ **constructor/type checker** catch at our call sites.

2. **`HandoffWireError`** ŌĆö raised when the event **cannot** be encoded for AGUI.

3. **`enforce_active_member_changed_agui_contract()`** ŌĆö before any handoff is returned:
   - Mirrors AgnoŌĆÖs `to_dict` / `content` resolution but **raises** instead of falling back to `None`
   - Runs the real **`_create_events_from_chunk` + `EventEncoder`** path
   - Fails if `CUSTOM.value` is missing or SSE would omit `"value"`

4. **`build_handoff_custom_event()`** ŌĆö only way we build handoff events; always runs enforcement.

So on a bad handoff, **`/agui` fails** (exception in the stream generator ŌåÆ run error), not a hollow `CUSTOM` frame.

Tests include **`test_handoff_without_content_fails_contract`** ŌĆö the old broken shape must raise `HandoffWireError`.

## Compile time vs runtime (straight talk)

- **Compile time (mypy):** You get safety on **`ActiveMemberChangedValue`** and our builders. You do **not** get compile-time proof that AgnoŌĆÖs `CustomEvent` honors that ŌĆö third-party, dynamic `__init__`.
- **Runtime (fail loud):** Every handoff is checked against the **actual** AGUI pipeline before yield. Regression = failed request, not wrong UI label.

ThatŌĆÖs the best you can do in Python without forking Agno: **typed contract on our side + mandatory wire check**, not ŌĆ£hope `to_dict()` works.ŌĆØ

## What we still donŌĆÖt control

Upstream Agno can still swallow errors for **other** custom events. We only harden **`active_member_changed`**. Fixing that globally means changing Agno (remove bare `except`, or require a typed payload protocol) ŌĆö out of scope unless you want an upstream PR.

**Bottom line:** You were correct ŌĆö that area had no real type safety and stupid fallbacks. Handoffs now **must** pass a typed value and a full AGUI encode check, or the operation **fails**. No logs as a substitute.

[REDACTED]

======

Layer	Change
Agno / Provisioner prompt

UI

</user_query>

Checking how agents are instructed to format chat output (markdown vs plain text).

[REDACTED]

Yes ŌĆö **by configuration our agents are told to use markdown**, and the UI **always renders assistant text as markdown**. Your RCA is right: the giant first line is almost certainly `# ŌĆ”` ŌåÆ `<h1>` inside `prose`, not a broken handoff or wrong member.

## What the backend does

Every Concierge-route member and the team leader set **`markdown=True`** (Provisioner included):

```107:107:agents/provisioner/build.py
        markdown=True,
```

```122:122:agents/concierge/build.py
        markdown=True,
```

In Agno, that injects into the model context (when thereŌĆÖs no structured output schema):

> **ŌĆ£Use markdown to format your answers.ŌĆØ**

So weŌĆÖre **actively asking** for markdown. `# Let's set up your new project` is expected model behavior, not an encoding bug. Langfuse concierge prompts donŌĆÖt say ŌĆ£no headings,ŌĆØ so the generic Agno line wins for chat tone.

`# Workflow State` in **system** instructions is separate ŌĆö thatŌĆÖs prompt composition for the model, not what the user sees in SSE (unless the model copies the style).

## What is *not* wrong

- AGUI / `active_member_changed` ŌĆö unrelated  
- ŌĆ£Intake form readyŌĆØ ŌĆö tool inline status, different component  
- Member label ŌĆö you already ruled that out  

## How to resolve (recommended: both layers)

### 1. UI (do this ŌĆö display owns rendering)

CopilotKit + `prose` will keep interpreting `#` as headings. Normalize **in the chat rail only**:

```css
/* example: match body size, modest weight steps */
.workspace-chat-view .prose :is(h1, h2, h3) {
  font-size: var(--text-sm) !important;
  font-weight: 600;
  margin: 0.5em 0 0.25em;
}
```

Or disable heading promotion for assistant messages (strip leading `#` in the renderer ŌĆö heavier-handed).

**Why:** Any agent, tool, or future prompt can still emit `#`. The UI should not let one markdown token blow up the layout.

### 2. Agents (reduce bad markdown at the source)

Pick one policy for **user-visible chat** (Concierge team + Provisioner):

| Approach | Change | Effect |
|--------|--------|--------|
| **A. Stricter (my preference for chat)** | `markdown=False` on concierge members, team, provisioner | Removes AgnoŌĆÖs ŌĆ£Use markdownŌĆ”ŌĆØ line |
| **B. Softer** | Keep `markdown=True` but add a Langfuse fragment, e.g. `rebaseai/chat-format`: ŌĆ£Plain sentences in chat. **Bold** ok. No `#` headings in short replies.ŌĆØ | Model may still slip; UI still needs (1) |

Use markdown/lists only where you really want structure (long reports in cards/docs), not in one-line provisioning intros.

**Do not** rely on logs or post-hoc stripping in the API unless you want to own a markdown sanitizer forever.

### 3. DonŌĆÖt fix only in Provisioner prompts

Provisioner is one offender; Concierge, Discovery, etc. share the same `markdown=True` pattern. Fix the **shared chat policy** once (constants + publish script + build sites).

## Practical sequence

1. **UI** ŌĆö heading rules in `copilot-theme.css` (or renderer) ŌåÆ fixes screenshot immediately for all agents.  
2. **Backend** ŌĆö `markdown=False` + optional `rebaseai/chat-format` in published prompts for concierge + provisioner.  
3. **Verify** ŌĆö replay provisioning turn; first deltas should not be `#` / `##` for a one-liner (or headings render at body size).

## Compile-time / fail-loud note

This is unrelated to the handoff wire contract: markdown is **content policy**, not type safety. Enforcement belongs in **prompts + optional lint on streamed deltas in tests**, not `HandoffWireError`.

---

**Bottom line:** Agents are encouraged to return markdown; the UI correctly renders it, but typography treats `#` as huge headings. Fix **UI prose headings for chat** (required) and **stop telling chat agents to ŌĆ£use markdownŌĆØ** without chat-specific rules (recommended). I can implement the backend half (`markdown=False` + chat-format fragment in `publish_concierge_prompts.py`) in this repo if you want that in the same PR as the UI CSS change.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
if we turn markdown off, will it stop doing all formatting? what about code blocks etc
</user_query>

Checking exactly what Agno's `markdown` flag controls versus what the model and UI still do.

[REDACTED]

**Short answer:** `markdown=False` only removes AgnoŌĆÖs ŌĆ£use markdownŌĆØ system nudge (and changes AgnoŌĆÖs **terminal** pretty-printing). It does **not** turn off markdown in the UI, and it does **not** block code blocks if the model still emits them.

## What `markdown=True` actually does in Agno

Two separate things:

| Layer | What `markdown` controls |
|--------|-------------------------|
| **Model instructions** | When `markdown=True` and no `output_schema`, Agno appends: *ŌĆ£Use markdown to format your answers.ŌĆØ* |
| **Agno CLI / Studio print** | Rich `Markdown` vs plain text when **printing** responses in a terminal |

It does **not**:

- Post-process or strip markdown from streamed text  
- Tell CopilotKit how to render  
- Enforce or forbid `#`, `**`, or ` ``` ` in the API payload  

Streamed content is still a plain string; the UI decides how to interpret it.

## If you set `markdown=False`

**Stops (mostly):**

- That generic ŌĆ£use markdownŌĆØ line ŌĆö models are less likely to lead with `# Title` for every reply.

**Does not stop:**

- The UI still running assistant text through a **markdown pipeline** (`prose`, etc.). Any ` ``` `, `**bold**`, or `#` in the stream still gets parsed if present.  
- The model from outputting markdown anyway (training habit). YouŌĆÖll still see code fences sometimes if the answer needs them.  
- **Code blocks** ŌĆö if the model writes:

  ` ```bash\nnpm install\n``` `

  CopilotKit will still render a code block. `markdown=False` doesnŌĆÖt disable that; it only removes the broad ŌĆ£format everything in markdownŌĆØ instruction.

**Agno-only side effect:** CLI/debug output wonŌĆÖt use Rich markdown rendering for that agent (usually irrelevant for `/agui`).

## What you probably want for chat

Not ŌĆ£no formatting,ŌĆØ but **chat-safe formatting**:

| OK in chat | Avoid in short chat lines |
|------------|---------------------------|
| Plain sentences | `#` / `##` headings (become huge `h1`/`h2` in `prose`) |
| `**bold**` for emphasis | Whole replies structured like docs |
| Fenced ` ```lang ` for commands, logs, URLs | |
| Short bullet lists when listing steps | |

ThatŌĆÖs **prompt policy** (Langfuse fragment), optionally plus `markdown=False` to drop AgnoŌĆÖs blunt default line.

Example fragment:

```text
Chat replies: plain sentences. Use **bold** sparingly. Use fenced code blocks only for commands, paths, or multi-line snippets. Do not use # markdown headings in conversational messages.
```

## UI vs backend

| Fix | Code blocks | Heading blow-up |
|-----|-------------|-----------------|
| UI: normalize `.prose h1ŌĆōh3` in chat rail | Unchanged (still work) | Fixed visually |
| `markdown=False` | Still work if model/prompt uses fences | Reduced, not guaranteed |
| Targeted prompt | You control when fences appear | Best for content |

**Recommendation:** Keep code blocks ŌĆö theyŌĆÖre useful for provisioning. Fix headings with **UI typography** (defensive) and **narrow prompt rules** (and optionally `markdown=False`), not by expecting `markdown=False` to ŌĆ£turn off formatting.ŌĆØ

If you want a single backend knob later, a small post-processor that only strips leading `#` lines in chat (not fences) is possible, but prompt + UI is usually enough.

[REDACTED]

Done.

## Branch & PR

- **Branch:** `feat/concierge-transparent-handoff`
- **PR:** https://github.com/trilogy-group/ab-agno/pull/185

## Commits

1. `feat(concierge): transparent handoff and AGUI wire contract` ŌĆö main implementation (35 files)
2. `fix(concierge): address Greptile review on handoff and prompts` ŌĆö review follow-ups

## CI (babysit)

| Check | Status |
|--------|--------|
| lint | pass |
| type | pass |
| test-unit | pass (502) |
| conventional-pr-title | pass |
| Semgrep | pass |

**Mergeable**, no open conflicts.

## Greptile follow-ups (2nd commit)

- Removed private `_create_events_from_chunk` import; validate via direct `AguiCustomEvent` + `EventEncoder`
- Wired `session_instructions_cache` for spine / idle / side-question fragments (stage still fetched per turn)
- Fixed redundant idle Langfuse fetch when `stage == "idle"`
- Design doc status ŌåÆ **Implemented (PR #185)**

## Before merge (manual)

Run once against Langfuse production:

```sh
uv run python scripts/publish_concierge_prompts.py
```

Restart `agentos-api` after merge so handoff + `markdown=False` land in the running container.

import os
import sys
from pathlib import Path

from dotenv import load_dotenv

load_dotenv(Path(".env"))

sys.path.insert(0, ".")
from scripts.publish_concierge_prompts import _all_prompts
from agents.concierge.constants import PRODUCT_INFO_PROMPT_NAME, PRODUCT_ROUTING_FRAME_PROMPT_NAME
from agents.langfuse_prompts import build_langfuse_client, prompt_text, load_langfuse_prompt_options

public = os.environ["LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY"].strip()
secret = os.environ["LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY"].strip()
base = os.environ["LANGFUSE_BASE_URL"].strip()
label = os.environ.get("LANGFUSE_PROMPT_LABEL", "production").strip() or "production"
opts = load_langfuse_prompt_options()

client = build_langfuse_client(public_key=public, secret_key=secret, base_url=base)

required = dict(_all_prompts())
# Runtime prompts outside publish script
runtime_extra = {
    PRODUCT_INFO_PROMPT_NAME: "completion suggestion chips (streaming.py)",
    PRODUCT_ROUTING_FRAME_PROMPT_NAME: "legacy routing frame (if referenced)",
}

all_names = sorted(set(required) | set(runtime_extra))

present: list[str] = []
missing: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
empty: list[str] = []

for name in all_names:

        body = prompt_text(client, name, label=label, cache_ttl_seconds=opts.cache_ttl_seconds)
        if not body.strip():
            empty.append(name)

print(f"LANGFUSE_BASE_URL={base}")
print(f"LABEL={label}")
print(f"publish_script_prompts={len(required)}")
print(f"present={len(present)} missing={len(missing)} empty={len(empty)}")
print()

    for name, err in missing:
        note = runtime_extra.get(name, "publish_concierge_prompts.py")
        print(f"  - {name}")

        print(f"    {err}")
if empty:

        print(f"  - {name}")
if not missing and not empty:
    print("All required prompts are present with non-empty bodies.")
else:

    if in_publish_missing:
        print()
        print(f"Missing from publish set: {len(in_publish_missing)} / {len(required)}")

All **35** prompts resolve with a non-empty body:

| Group | Count | Status |
|--------|------:|--------|

- **Router / Concierge:** `ux/concierge-router`, `ux/concierge`, `rebaseai/concierge/stage-router`, `rebaseai/concierge/idle`, `rebaseai/concierge/side_question`

- **Reimaginer:** spine, mechanics, stages `idle`, `scanning`, `proposing`, `iterating`, `awaiting_pick`, `picked`

- **Provisioner:** spine, mechanics, stages `idle`, `intake`, `bootstrapping`, `awaiting_user_action`, `deployed`

### Legacy monolithic prompts (not used by `prompt_composer`, but still there)

## Action

No publish run required for missing prompts. If you change prompt text in the repo, run:

uv run python scripts/publish_concierge_prompts.py

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Copy retired Concierge monolithic Langfuse prompts to *-legacy names (production label)."""

from __future__ import annotations

import os
import sys
from pathlib import Path

from dotenv import load_dotenv

_REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
if str(_REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO_ROOT))
sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO_ROOT / "src"))

load_dotenv(_REPO_ROOT / ".env")

# Monolithic prompts superseded by rebaseai/*/spine + stage/* composition.
LEGACY_RENAMES: dict[str, str] = {
    "ux/discovery": "ux/discovery-legacy",
    "ux/reimaginer": "ux/reimaginer-legacy",
    "ux/planner": "ux/planner-legacy",
    "ux/provisioner": "ux/provisioner-legacy",
    "rebaseai/product-routing-frame": "rebaseai/product-routing-frame-legacy",
}


def main() -> int:
    from agents.langfuse_prompts import build_langfuse_client, load_langfuse_prompt_options, prompt_text

    public = os.environ.get("LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY", "").strip()
    secret = os.environ.get("LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY", "").strip()
    base = os.environ.get("LANGFUSE_BASE_URL", "").strip()
    if not public or not secret or not base:
        print("Missing LANGFUSE_* env vars", file=sys.stderr)
        return 1

    label = os.environ.get("LANGFUSE_PROMPT_LABEL", "production").strip() or "production"
    opts = load_langfuse_prompt_options()
    client = build_langfuse_client(public_key=public, secret_key=secret, base_url=base)

    from langfuse import Langfuse

    lf = Langfuse(public_key=public, secret_key=secret, host=base.rstrip("/"))

    for old_name, new_name in sorted(LEGACY_RENAMES.items()):
        body = prompt_text(
            client,
            old_name,
            label=label,
            cache_ttl_seconds=opts.cache_ttl_seconds,
        )
        lf.create_prompt(
            name=new_name,
            prompt=body,
            labels=[label],
            commit_message=f"archive legacy prompt (was {old_name})",
        )
        print(f"published {new_name} <- {old_name} ({label})")

    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

"""Validated environment for the Concierge route team."""

from __future__ import annotations

import os
from dataclasses import dataclass
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from agents.concierge.constants import (
    CONCIERGE_MEMBER_LANGFUSE_PROMPT_NAME,
    CONCIERGE_ROUTER_LANGFUSE_PROMPT_NAME,
)

_DEFAULT_TERMINAL_TTYD_URL = "http://localhost:7681/"
_DEFAULT_DEMO_TERMINAL_BASE_URL = "https://demo.rebase.ai/terminal"


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class ConciergeSettings:
    langfuse_public_key: str
    langfuse_secret_key: str
    langfuse_base_url: str
    router_langfuse_prompt_name: str
    concierge_member_langfuse_prompt_name: str
    default_terminal_url: str
    demo_terminal_base_url: str


def _require_env(name: str) -> str:
    value = os.environ.get(name, "").strip()
    if not value:
        raise ValueError(f"Missing required environment variable: {name}")
    return value


def _optional_env(name: str, default: str) -> str:
    value = os.environ.get(name, "").strip()
    return value or default


def load_concierge_settings() -> ConciergeSettings:
    """Load Concierge settings; fail fast when Langfuse credentials are missing."""
    public_key = _require_env("LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY")
    secret_key = _require_env("LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY")
    base_url = _require_env("LANGFUSE_BASE_URL")

    parsed = urlparse(base_url)
    if parsed.scheme not in {"http", "https"} or not parsed.netloc:
        raise ValueError("LANGFUSE_BASE_URL must be an http(s) URL with a host")

    router_prompt = _optional_env(
        "CONCIERGE_ROUTER_LANGFUSE_PROMPT_NAME", CONCIERGE_ROUTER_LANGFUSE_PROMPT_NAME
    )
    concierge_member_prompt = _optional_env(
        "CONCIERGE_MEMBER_LANGFUSE_PROMPT_NAME",
        CONCIERGE_MEMBER_LANGFUSE_PROMPT_NAME,
    )

    return ConciergeSettings(
        langfuse_public_key=public_key,
        langfuse_secret_key=secret_key,
        langfuse_base_url=base_url.rstrip("/"),
        router_langfuse_prompt_name=router_prompt,
        concierge_member_langfuse_prompt_name=concierge_member_prompt,
        default_terminal_url=_optional_env(
            "CONCIERGE_TERMINAL_TTYD_URL", _DEFAULT_TERMINAL_TTYD_URL
        ),
        demo_terminal_base_url=_optional_env(
            "CONCIERGE_DEMO_TERMINAL_BASE_URL",
            _DEFAULT_DEMO_TERMINAL_BASE_URL,
        ),
    )

"""Build the provisioner agent."""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import Callable

from agno.agent import Agent
from agno.db.postgres import PostgresDb

from agents.devpod.provisioner import DevpodProvisioner
from agents.devpod.settings import (
    DevpodSettings,
    WebTerminalSettings,
    load_devpod_settings,
    load_web_terminal_settings,
)
from agents.devpod.ssh_client import DevpodSshClient
from agents.devpod.web_terminal_service import WebTerminalService
from agents.devpod.workspace_service import DevpodWorkspaceService
from agents.provisioner.bootstrap_service import (
    ProvisionerBootstrapService,
    build_supabase_client,
)
from agents.provisioner.constants import PROVISIONER_AGENT_ID
from agents.provisioner.prerequisite_watcher import PrerequisiteWatcher
from agents.provisioner.render_tools import provisioner_render_toolkit
from agents.provisioner.settings import ProvisionerSettings, load_provisioner_settings
from agents.provisioner.streaming import wrap_provisioner_arun
from agents.provisioner.tools import build_provisioner_tools
from agents.provisioner.transcripts import provisioner_transcript_root
from agno_platform.models.presets import MODEL_MEDIUM


def build_provisioner_agent(
    db: PostgresDb,
    *,
    instructions: str | Callable[..., str],
    settings: ProvisionerSettings | None = None,
    devpod_settings: DevpodSettings | None = None,
    web_terminal_settings: WebTerminalSettings | None = None,
) -> Agent:
    """Build the Provisioner agent. ``instructions`` must come from the composition root."""
    resolved_settings = settings or load_provisioner_settings()
    resolved_devpod = devpod_settings or load_devpod_settings()
    resolved_terminal = web_terminal_settings or load_web_terminal_settings()

    provisioner = DevpodProvisioner()
    ssh_client = DevpodSshClient()
    transcript_root = provisioner_transcript_root()

    workspace_service = DevpodWorkspaceService(
        settings=resolved_devpod,
        provisioner=provisioner,
        ssh_client=ssh_client,
        transcript_root=transcript_root,
    )
    bootstrap_service = ProvisionerBootstrapService(
        settings=resolved_settings,
        devpod_settings=resolved_devpod,
        ssh_client=ssh_client,
        supabase_client=build_supabase_client(resolved_settings),
        transcript_root=transcript_root,
    )
    web_terminal_service = WebTerminalService(
        devpod_settings=resolved_devpod,
        terminal_settings=resolved_terminal,
        ssh_client=ssh_client,
        transcript_root=transcript_root,
    )
    prerequisite_watcher = PrerequisiteWatcher(
        settings=resolved_settings,
        devpod_settings=resolved_devpod,
        ssh_client=ssh_client,
        transcript_root=transcript_root,
    )
    coordinator_tools = build_provisioner_tools(
        workspace_service=workspace_service,
        bootstrap_service=bootstrap_service,
        web_terminal_service=web_terminal_service,
        prerequisite_watcher=prerequisite_watcher,
    )

    agent = Agent(
        id=PROVISIONER_AGENT_ID,
        name="Provisioner",
        model=MODEL_MEDIUM,
        db=db,
        tools=[provisioner_render_toolkit, coordinator_tools],
        instructions=instructions,
        add_datetime_to_context=True,
        add_history_to_context=True,
        read_chat_history=True,
        num_history_runs=5,
        add_session_state_to_context=True,
        markdown=False,
    )
    wrap_provisioner_arun(agent)
    return agent

def load_provisioner_settings() -> ProvisionerSettings:
    """Load provisioner settings; fail fast when required values are missing."""
    langfuse_base = _require_env("LANGFUSE_BASE_URL")
    langfuse_parsed = urlparse(langfuse_base)
    if langfuse_parsed.scheme not in {"http", "https"} or not langfuse_parsed.netloc:
        raise ValueError("LANGFUSE_BASE_URL must be an http(s) URL with a host")

    supabase_disabled = _parse_bool("PROVISION_SUPABASE_BOOTSTRAP_DISABLED", default=False)

"""Unit tests for Concierge Langfuse prompt fetch."""

from __future__ import annotations

from unittest.mock import MagicMock

from agents.concierge.langfuse_prompts import fetch_langfuse_prompt
from agents.langfuse_prompts import prompt_text
from tests.unit.concierge.conftest import make_concierge_settings


class _PromptWithCompile:
    def compile(self, **kwargs: str) -> str:
        return f"terminal={kwargs['default_terminal_url']}"


def test_prompt_text_substitutes_mustache_variables() -> None:
    client = MagicMock()
    client.get_prompt.return_value = "ttyd at {{default_terminal_url}}"
    body = prompt_text(
        client,
        "ux/concierge",
        label="production",
        cache_ttl_seconds=900,
        variables={"default_terminal_url": "http://localhost:7681/"},
    )
    assert body == "ttyd at http://localhost:7681/"


def test_fetch_concierge_member_prompt_passes_variables() -> None:
    client = MagicMock()
    client.get_prompt.return_value = _PromptWithCompile()
    settings = make_concierge_settings()
    body = fetch_langfuse_prompt(
        client,
        settings.concierge_member_langfuse_prompt_name,
        settings=settings,
    )
    assert body == "terminal=http://localhost:7681/"

    assert settings.demo_terminal_base_url == "https://demo.rebase.ai/terminal"

content.text:
Removed the one-off Langfuse tooling and the inlined prompt bodies:

**Deleted**
- `scripts/publish_concierge_prompts.py` ŌĆö full prompt text lived here (~11k of copy-paste bodies)
- `scripts/rename_legacy_concierge_prompts.py` ŌĆö one-off `*-legacy` rename publisher

**Updated**
- `example.env` ŌĆö comment now points at Langfuse `*-legacy` names only; no script reference

**Unchanged (by design)**
- Runtime code only references Langfuse **names** (`agents/concierge/constants.py`, `prompt_composer.py`)
- `agents/concierge/DESIGN-transparent-handoff.md` still has example prompt prose for the handoff design; say if you want that trimmed to names-only too

`./scripts/validate.sh` still passes (501 tests). Prompt content stays in Langfuse only; nothing in `scripts/` pushes or copies prompts anymore.
